hypothesis: If a computational framework combining fragment-based polymer generation, DFT-refined force field parameters, and Bayesian optimization with pre-trained molecular representations is applied to the chemical space of polyamines, then it will accelerate the discovery of high-performance solid-state CO2 capture materials by efficiently identifying structures with superior adsorption capacity within a limited simulation budget compared to exhaustive or random screening approaches, because the surrogate model-guided search exploits structure-property relationships encoded in pre-trained embeddings to prioritize the most promising candidates.
